title: Gently Spiced Syrian Lamb Stew
description: A malty, molten, and comforting Syrian dish featuring Thai red curry paste, perfect for a sick day, with a low-carb friendly twist.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, and coriander, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the Thai red curry paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 2.5 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
